tldr: NATS and Lob solve different problems — NATS is an open-source pub/sub and message-queue system, Lob is a hosted direct-mail API — so this is not a like-for-like migration but a refactor of any messaging code that was actually trying to send notifications to end users.
why_switch: Lob exposes a managed REST API with SDKs — no broker to operate
Direct-mail and address-verification capabilities NATS does not provide
Removes the ops burden of running NATS clusters (only relevant if that's the real cost)
Better fit if the underlying need is reaching end users, not internal pub/sub
what_to_export: Inventory of NATS subjects and consumers that map to user-facing notifications
Recipient / address data sources used by those flows
Templates or copy used for outbound communications
Monitoring and dashboards tied to NATS
List of internal flows that must stay on NATS
export_method: NATS does not offer a vendor 'export' — replay or drain JetStream streams via the NATS CLI or client libraries if needed, and capture subject/consumer topology from your own configuration. Consult NATS's official docs for current tooling.
import_method: On Lob, create an account, generate API keys, and call its REST API or SDK from the services that previously published to NATS for user-facing messaging. Keep internal pub/sub on NATS where appropriate. Consult Lob's official docs for current setup steps.

concept_mapping.note: Lob delivers events back via webhooks
Lob is not a stream store

gotchas: Categories differ — do NOT replace internal microservice pub/sub with Lob
Lob is request/response, not streaming; you lose backpressure and replay semantics
Per-message costs replace the fixed cost of running NATS
Latency and ordering guarantees are not equivalent
If you were using JetStream for durability, design a new persistence story before cutover
Plan a hybrid: NATS for internal events, Lob only where end-user mail is needed

email.subject: Refactoring user-facing notifications from NATS to Lob
email.body:
Hi team,

We're scoping a partial move from NATS to Lob — only for the flows where NATS was being used to drive end-user messaging. Internal pub/sub stays on NATS.

Scope: identify the user-facing subjects and consumers, design a request/response path against Lob's API, and wire webhooks back into our handlers. Estimated 1-3 days.

Flagging up front: these are different categories, so this is a refactor, not a swap. Details in the ticket.

Thanks
